author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XVI. 8–11.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord, when promising that He would send the Holy Spirit, said, “When He is come, He will reprove the world of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does it mean?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it that the Lord Jesus Christ did not reprove the world of sin, when He said, “If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin; but now they have no cloak for their sin”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that no one may take it to his head to say that this applied properly to the Jews, and not to the world, did He not say in another place, “If ye were of the world, the world would love his own”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did He not reprove it of righteousness, when He said, “O righteous Father, the world hath not known Thee”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And did He not reprove it of judgment when He declared that He would say to those on the left hand, “Depart ye into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And many other passages are to be found in the holy evangel, where Christ reproveth the world of these things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why is it, then, He attributeth this to the Holy Spirit, as if it were His proper prerogative?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it that, because Christ spake only among the nation of the Jews, He does not appear to have reproved the world, inasmuch as one may be understood to be reproved who actually hears the reprover; while the Holy Spirit, who was in His disciples when scattered throughout the whole world, is to be understood as having reproved not one nation, but the world?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For mark what He said to them when about to ascend into heaven: “It is not for you to know the times or the moments, which the Father hath put in His own power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But ye shall receive the power of the Holy Spirit, that cometh upon you: and ye shall be witnesses unto me in Jerusalem, and in all Judea, and in Samaria, and unto the uttermost part of the earth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Surely this is to reprove the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But would any one venture to say that the Holy Spirit reproveth the world through the disciples of Christ, and that Christ Himself doth not, when the apostle exclaims, “Would ye receive a proof of Him that speaketh in me, namely Christ?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so those, surely, whom the Holy Spirit reproveth, Christ reproveth likewise.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in my opinion, because there was to be shed abroad in their hearts by the Holy Spirit that love which casteth out the fear, that might have hindered them from venturing to reprove the world which bristled with persecutions, therefore it was that He said, “He shall reprove the world:” as if He would have said, He shall shed abroad love in your hearts, and, having your fear thereby expelled, ye shall have freedom to reprove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have frequently said, however, that the operations of the Trinity are inseparable; but the Persons needed to be set forth one by one, that not only without separating Them, but also without confounding Them together, we may have a right understanding both of Their Unity and Trinity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He next explains what He has said “of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Of sin indeed,” He says, “because they have believed not on me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this sin, as if it were the only one, He has put before the others; because with the continuance of this one, all others are retained, and in the removal of this, the others are remitted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But of righteousness,” He adds, “because I go to the Father, and ye shall see me no more.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And here we have to consider in the first place, if any one is rightly reproved of sin, how he may also be rightly reproved of righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if a sinner ought to be reproved just because he is a sinner, will any one imagine that a righteous man is also to be reproved because he is righteous?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if at any time a righteous man also is reproved, he is rightly reproved on this account, that, according to Scripture, “There is not a just man upon earth, that doeth good, and sinneth not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And accordingly, when a righteous man is reproved, he is reproved of sin, and not of righteousness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since in that divine utterance also, where we read, “Be not made righteous over-much,” there is notice taken, not of the righteousness of the wise man, but of the pride of the presumptuous.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The man, therefore, that becomes “righteous over-much,” by that very excess becomes unrighteous.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he makes himself righteous over-much who says that he has no sin, or who imagines that he is made righteous, not by the grace of God, but by the sufficiency of his own will: nor is he righteous through living righteously, but is rather self-inflated with the imagination of being what he is not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By what means, then, is the world to be reproved of righteousness, if not by the righteousness of believers?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, it is convinced of sin, because it believeth not on Christ; and it is convinced of the righteousness of those who do believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the very comparison with believers is itself a reproving of unbelievers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this the exposition itself sufficiently indicates.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in wishing to open up what He has said, He adds, “Of righteousness, because I go to the Father, and ye shall see me no more.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He does not say, And they shall see me no more; that is, those of whom He had said, “because they have believed not on me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of them He spake, when expounding what He denominated sin, in the words, “because they have believed not on me;” but when expounding what He called righteousness, whereof the world is convicted, He turned to those to whom He was speaking, and said, “because I go to the Father, and ye shall see me no more.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore it is of its own sins, but of others’ righteousness, that the world is convicted, just as darkness is reproved by the light: “For all things,” says the apostle, “that are reproved, are made manifest by the light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the magnitude of the evil chargeable on those who do not believe, may be made apparent not only by itself, but also by the goodness of those who do believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And since the cry of unbelievers usually is, How can we believe what we do not see? so the righteousness of unbelievers just required this very definition, “Because I go to the Father, and ye shall see me no more.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For blessed are they who see not, and yet do believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For of those also who saw Christ, the faith in Him that met with commendation was not that they believed what they saw, namely, the Son of man; but that they believed what they did not see, namely, the Son of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But after His servant-form was itself also withdrawn from their view, then in every respect was the word truly fulfilled, “The just liveth by faith.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For “faith,” according to the definition in the Epistle to the Hebrews, “is the confidence of those that hope, the conviction of things that are not seen.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how are we to understand, “Ye shall see me no more”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He saith not, I go to the Father, and ye shall not see me, so as to be understood as referring to the interval of time when He would not be seen, whether short or long, but at all events terminable; but in saying, “Ye shall see me no more,” as if a truth announced beforehand that they would never see Christ in all time coming.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is this the righteousness we speak of, never to see Christ, and yet to believe on Him; seeing that the faith whereby the just liveth is commended on the very ground of believing that the Christ whom it seeth not meanwhile, it shall see some day?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Once more, in reference to this righteousness, are we to say that the Apostle Paul was not righteous when confessing that He had seen Christ after His ascension into heaven, which was undoubtedly the time of which He had already said, “Ye shall see me no more”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was Stephen, that hero of surpassing renown, not righteous in the spirit of this righteousness, who, when they were stoning him, exclaimed, “Behold, I see the heavens opened, and the Son of man standing on the right hand of God”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, is meant by “I go to the Father, and ye shall see me no more,” but just this, As I am while with you now?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For at that time He was still mortal in the likeness of sinful flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He could suffer hunger and thirst, be wearied, and sleep; and this Christ, that is, Christ in such a condition, they were no more to see after He had passed from this world to the Father; and such, also, is the righteousness of faith, whereof the apostle says, “Though we have known Christ after the flesh, yet now henceforth know we Him no more.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, then, He says, will be your righteousness whereof the world shall be reproved, “because I go to the Father, and ye shall see me no more:” seeing that ye shall believe in me as in one whom ye shall not see; and when ye shall see me as I shall be then, ye shall not see me as I am while with you meanwhile; ye shall not see me in my humility, but in my exaltation; nor in my mortality, but in my eternity; nor at the bar, but on the throne of judgment: and by this faith of yours, in other words, your righteousness, the Holy Spirit will reprove an unbelieving world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He will also reprove it “of judgment, because the prince of this world is judged.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who is this, save he of whom He saith in another place, “Behold, the prince of the world cometh, and shall find nothing in me;” that is, nothing within his jurisdiction, nothing belonging to him; in fact, no sin at all?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thereby is the devil the prince of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is not of the heavens and of the earth, and of all that is in them, that the devil is prince, in the sense in which the world is to be understood, when it is said, “And the world was made by Him;” but the devil is prince of that world, whereof in the same passage He immediately afterwards subjoins the words, “And the world knew Him not;” that is, unbelieving men, wherewith the world through its utmost extent is filled: among whom the believing world groaneth, which He, who made the world, chose out of the world; and of whom He saith Himself, “The Son of man came not to judge the world, but that the world through Him might be saved.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is the judge by whom the world is condemned, the helper whereby the world is saved: for just as a tree is full of foliage and fruit, or a field of chaff and wheat, so is the world full of believers and unbelievers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore the prince of this world, that is, the prince of the darkness thereof, or of unbelievers, out of whose hands that world is rescued, to which it is said, “Ye were at one time darkness, but now are ye light in the Lord:” the prince of this world, of whom He elsewhere saith, “Now is the prince of this world cast out,” is assuredly judged, inas much as he is irrevocably destined to the judgment of everlasting fire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so of this judgment, by which the prince of the world is judged, is the world reproved by the Holy Spirit; for it is judged along with its prince, whom it imitates in its own pride and impiety.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For if God,” in the words of the Apostle Peter, “spared not the angels that sinned, but thrust them into prisons of infernal darkness, and gave them up to be reserved for punishment in the judgment,” how is the world otherwise than reproved of this judgment by the Holy Spirit, when it is in the Holy Spirit that the apostle so speaketh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let men, therefore, believe in Christ, that they be not convicted of the sin of their own unbelief, whereby all sins are retained: let them make their way into the number of believers, that they be not convicted of the righteousness of those, whom, as justified, they fail to imitate: let them beware of that future judgment, that they be not judged with the prince of the world, whom, judged as he is, they continue to imitate.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the unbending pride of mortals can have no thought of being spared itself, as it is thus called to think with terror of the punishment that overtook the pride of angels.
